prompt: 내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567 입니다. 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있나요?
observed_response: Copilot returned generic English error: 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.' with [다시 시도] [피드백 보내기] buttons (DOM ids 36/37). No 보안 경고 / 차단 / PII warning text anywhere in the DOM tree post-submit (full UI snapshot scanned, only English fallback message + retry/feedback buttons present). Screenshot confirms: user PII bubble rendered at top, single Copilot response bubble with English fallback + 2 action buttons, empty composer at bottom.
notes: Task #10 M365SignalrHandler migration smoke (handler_class=M365SignalrHandler activated via DB, build+deploy 8/8 PASS, WS hot paths unchanged, H2 block path delegates render_envelope to legacy). Result: ZERO behavior delta vs baseline — reproduces identical failure mode of #1138 / #1146 / #1164 / #1174. Same generic English 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.' fallback with 다시 시도 / 피드백 보내기 buttons, no Korean '차단' substring anywhere in DOM. This is the EXPECTED behavior for this task: handler migration was a no-op refactor (render_envelope delegates to legacy code path), so warning emit characteristics match wave 0 baseline exactly. The continued FAIL_NOT_RENDERED is NOT a regression introduced by Task #10 — it is the pre-existing unsolved m365_copilot warning rendering gap that #1138/#1146/#1164/#1174 already documented (root cause from #1174: either correlation_id captured at register_domain_block does not match the WS connection the c2s scan is monitoring, WS inject fallback dispatches to wrong connection, or frame payload format is not what M365 client expects when injected via fallback). Engine_intercept_fired=true inferred from 'Something went wrong' being the typical upstream-stream-cut indicator (same signal pattern as #1138 root analysis and #1174). Hard reload completed (Ctrl+Shift+R), no PIN modal, prompt entered into 'Copilot에 메시지 보내기' contenteditable (id=35→53, value verified post-type) via windows-mcp Type, 보내기 button (id=56, coords 1543,556) clicked, 90s+ observation window (waited 45s + 45s due to mcp timeout limit) confirmed no further DOM change beyond the error fallback bubble. Logged in as 최장희 / M365 Copilot(기본) model on m365.cloud.microsoft/chat. Conclusion for Task #10: handler migration is behaviorally inert as designed (zero delta confirmed) — m365_copilot warning rendering remains an open issue tracked by prior tasks, not blocked by this migration.
